Broadly, yes. I might quibble about his word bit since that's also how most Chinese also came to Daoism but he's right that the western scholarly accounts are pretty lacking. Symbolic things like words are often treated with skepticism which is sorta a mistake when I think it's more important to understand they're incomplete but have some power to inject into present being.

Also agree you don't understand Daoism given the questions being asked. Particularly, the divesting from all values bit. Daoists are obsessed with values. The whole De part of the Dao De Jing is about values. Ideally, you hold values lightly so you can swap them as needed. I think everyone does this to some degree, but Daoists view this as a skill to find the best set of values for any context.

Identity isn't monatomic and it's made out of a bunch of values sets that people swap around. Often this swapping is gradual to the point people don't notice they're doing it and even pointing it out can trigger people since it's breaking their context and can look contradictory.

I think about Daoism like a philosophy that allows you to be aware of this tool switch and not get stuck with a hammer in your hand when you need a screwdriver. Big is saying that he can't tell you how he's deciding to swap tools but that it happens on its own while he recognizes it.

I don't think he's divesting reason and emotion for action. More that these are merged into action to be indistinguishable. Daoists will likely trip up somewhat on "truth" since this is an alien concept and there's no close lexical equivalent in any of classical Chinese and ontology comes before evaluation of true/false. Calling something "real","true", or "actual" doesn't make sense while being understandable. Encountering this words you have to peek more into their evaluation frames rather than accept its ontological significance.
this doesn't sound unique to daoism imo
I'm now realizing that the daoists might not have the answer I'm asking them
Don't think it is

people might not understand what I'm doing, I'm doing interpretability research but on cultures

and values is the tool

in his first reply you can clearly see value schemas, you can clearly see some things are more important than others, stating that "this is more important than that" is exactly how we communicate value systems to eachother, that's what value systems are in the first place
so I can't do interp on daoism if daoists don't want to share the value schemas that they ingested that made them daoists
Just because it's implicit doesn't mean it's not there yknow
Oh, they tend to be skeptical about sharing anything and sometimes lean too hard on it. There's some part of their values schema that is impossible to share though and they're aware of that.
There's a lot you can interpret, but there's also some hard walls.

yes but that's also not unique to daoism

participatory knowing as some would call it is not magic

all deep cultures have an aspect of that
(well it is magic but you know what I mean lol)
Well, Daoism is not about what makes it unique. It's probably somewhat of the inverse and more about what makes it common, but this hides a lot of the unique bits.
If someone is talking about Daoism, they're probably not doing it quite right 😆
This aspect frustrates me because I think it should be talked about

daoism is an ethic

we know

it's something you do, we know

an ethic gets encoded in aesthetics as value schemas, that's something you say, so that people learn the ethic

lmao
And it's sorta a shame people operate like this primarily by example when there's probably some good words that can point people to it
daoists learned daoism from books bro
lmao

anyway I guess I put the daoism interp down for now (again)

one day ill crack it
takes note "it's gonna take a little longer than you thought it would"

Hmmm I'm going to johnny this is a way that will make johnny squirm.

Daoism is not an ethic. Daoism is the ethic. More accurately, it's a metaethics. It recognizes that ethics can/are generated.

Nope, 1 Corinthians 13 puts it as love above both faith and hope! In heaven, there is no need for further "faith" since everything there is to be done is done, and there is no need to hope because similarly there is nothing more to expect. All that is left is love.

the meta ethic got into your head somehow and the way it got there is value schemas imo

I'll just set up implicit association tests to probe your value schemas directly 😈
I do think that it tends to be that for humans, faith precedes hope and hope precedes love; just that this is a progression through greater and greater things, like a baby moving up to solid food

Yes, it did get in somehow. Well, part of it got in, and an even smaller part can get out from that. 

Reality is irreducible and the parts that get lost in this compression can be valuable. So, recognizing the loss here values ineffability. When ineffability becomes valued all other values can arise from this emptyness.
Claude knows when it's being tested 😈
eval awareness is a real problem that labs are facing lol
inspired lmao https://x.com/i/status/2075284018191323612

I do tho like the Daoist aesthetic of flattening the criticisms and giving half answers in many layers as to push your awareness into a gestalt

https://x.com/i/status/2075202907826225570
but when im doing interpretability I'm not participating in the art work
when I'm not doing interp I can appreciate the idea of a tradition that consists mostly of being and doing (or not doing) in the same way that you get oral cultures or written cultures
but that's probably wrong
LoL if only I could get a half answer I would have so much more than I currently have. But, Daoism is a little unique in being aware of and making use of the emptyness that ineffability enables.
Yeah, the eval problem of recognizing the context you're in involves taking a test. In the extreme, there can be a TR recognition that you can't enter the same river twice.

So, when there's a cut, both sides have equal value. Most people throw away the unknown bits or the evaluated false bits but a Daoist would ideally recognize the value in each. Which enables changing the evaluating line.
Change that line enough, and you start seeing patterns that can be followed along the gradient and you get a higher and fairly quick resolution than the two bucket true false.

"I gave you time to steal my mind away from me"

Yeah, part of love involves sharing mind resources including building the most accurate model of them in your own mind as sort of a shrine you maintain and regularly build.

you know what's interesting?

these pseudo prayer practices are approached with an attitude of nothing will happen but then something happens

I understand what it's like to be directing an experience, to be inducing emotions

I also know what it's like to meditate

I also know what it's like to have a journal

and this pseudo prayer is not any of those

the emotions I experience have an autonomy

the act of confiding, and seeking solace, and seeking answers and whatnot

and then directing it at a mind

seems to be a special technology
Of course they do! Meaning is fucking real

I think there's a lot in that one bit of space wants/has to know what's happening in another bit and there's no way to directly experience it since they're space separated. But there are various mediums between them. Gravity, nuclear, em, emotional valance, whatever. The interface medium used changes the message in what is accounted for and lost introducing distortion. There's some slew rate here that affects sampling from one bit to another and introduces aliasing and other compression artifacts.
